generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors internal temperature of the incubator's styrofoam container for feedback control and model validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary internal temperature sensor providing redundant measurement for fault detection and improved thermal model calibration"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures ambient external temperature to account for environmental influences on the incubator's thermal dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:desc "Analog temperature readings from the primary internal sensor transmitted at high frequency for real-time monitoring"
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Analog temperature readings from the secondary internal sensor for cross-validation and redundancy"
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Analog ambient temperature readings used to model environmental influences on the incubator's thermal behavior"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified lumped parameter model with 2 free parameters representing basic thermal dynamics of the incubator"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced lumped parameter model with 4 free parameters accounting for additional thermal phenomena like heatbed inertia"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance ThermalDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential equation-based model implementing heat transfer physics for the incubator's styrofoam container, heatbed, and fan"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance SensorTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series collection of all temperature sensor readings (internal and external) for model calibration and monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageEnabler, StreamProcessingEnabler
]

instance ModelPredictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Outputs from thermal dynamic models representing simulated temperature profiles and system responses"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance ControlSignalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized control signals (PWM for heatbed, fan speed) generated by supervisory controllers"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuatorInterfaceEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Parameter estimation algorithms (least squares, Bayesian inference) that reconcile model predictions with sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical simulation tools (OMNeT++, custom Python scripts) reproducing incubator behavior in virtual environment"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService, WhatIfAnalysisService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB) storing sensor telemetry for historical analysis and model calibration"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance StreamProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Stream processing framework (Apache Kafka, Node-RED) handling high-frequency sensor data for low-latency monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization libraries (CVXPY, Gurobi) solving constrained problems for energy-efficient thermal regulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveControlService, EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard tools (Grafana, Matplotlib) and 3D modeling (Blender API) for real-time metrics and thermal gradient visualization"
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportService, WhatIfAnalysisService
]

instance ActuatorInterfaceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT/CoAP protocols enabling lightweight communication between Raspberry Pi and actuators (heatbed, fan)"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlExecutionService
]

instance FaultDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Statistical process control and machine learning (Isolation Forest, LSTM autoencoders) for anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection and processing of sensor data to provide current system state and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, ThermalDeviationInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of dynamic thermal models to reproduce and predict incubator behavior under current/perturbed conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalPredictionInsight, SystemResponseInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of model parameters using calibration algorithms to maintain synchronization with physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identification of anomalies in sensor data or control signals using statistical and machine learning techniques"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, CorrectiveActionTrigger
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters (PID tuning, MPC) based on real-time conditions and model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlOptimizationInsight, ActuatorCommandAction
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimization of heating profiles to minimize energy consumption while maintaining temperature constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, PowerAdjustmentAction
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provision of actionable insights through visualizations and what-if analyses for engineers and operators"
    DTDFVocab:provides DecisionRecommendationInsight
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation of hypothetical scenarios to assess impact of environmental changes or system modifications"

]

instance ControlExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

]

]

// C1: System Components
instance IncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical incubator system consisting of styrofoam container, heatbed, fan, and temperature sensors"

]

]

instance ThermalControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system implementing PID/MPC controllers for adaptive thermal regulation"

]

    base:desc "Operational environment including ambient conditions affecting the incubator's thermal performance"
    base:contains ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance IncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Complete cyber-physical system integrating physical twin, digital twin, and control components"

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via a **Raspberry Pi-based controller** that executes commands to regulate the incubator’s temperature. The control flows include **temperature setpoint adjustments** and **actuation commands** (e.g., enabling/disabling the heatbed or fan) to achieve the desired thermal conditions. Command types are likely **discrete (on/off) or continuous (PWM-based)** for the heatbed, with message formats adhering to a **cyber-physical communication protocol** (e.g., serial, I2C, or network-based). Validation and authentication mechanisms are not explicitly described, but the system implies **closed-loop control** where the DT’s monitors (e.g., fault detectors or state estimators) validate commands before execution. Feedback systems include **real-time sensor data** (internal/external temperatures) looped back to the DT for recalibration and adaptive control. Conflict resolution is inferred to occur via **supervisory algorithms** (as referenced in Document 1) that detect violations (e.g., unsafe temperatures) and trigger corrective actions, such as adjusting the heatbed power or alerting human operators. Command execution confirmation is likely implicit via **sensor feedback** (e.g., stabilized internal temperature) or explicit acknowledgments from the PT’s controller."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study operates with **real-time synchronization** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its Digital Twin (DT) to ensure accurate temperature regulation. The system relies on **sensor data collection** (temperature sensors inside and outside the insulated container) at a frequency determined by the controller (Raspberry Pi) and the calibration algorithms, which update the models with the best estimate of parameters. While the documents do not specify exact synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, the **calibration process** implies iterative updates to models (e.g., differential equations for thermal dynamics) to reflect the PT's current state, suggesting a **sub-second to millisecond-scale** temporal granularity for critical control loops. The DT must account for **temporal delays** in sensor data transmission and model simulation to maintain predictive accuracy, particularly for dynamic processes like thermal inertia of the heatbed. The use of **state estimators** (e.g., for temperature prediction) further necessitates low-latency data processing to align with the PT's operational time scale, though exact thresholds are not quantified in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **closed-loop cyber-physical architecture**, where the DT integrates **three core components**:
  1. **Data Layer**: Real-time sensor inputs (temperature inside/outside the box) streamed from the PT.
  2. **Model Layer**: Two thermal simulation models (with 2 or 4 free parameters) representing the PT’s physics, calibrated via algorithms to minimize prediction error.
  3. **Algorithm Layer**: Includes **calibration routines** (parameter estimation), **state estimators** (temperature prediction), and **supervisory controllers** (corrective actions for deviations).
  The orchestration adheres to a **feedback-driven pattern**: the DT continuously compares PT data with model predictions, adjusts parameters, and triggers actions (e.g., heatbed activation) via the Raspberry Pi controller. Resource management is implicit, with computational demands concentrated on **model calibration** and **real-time monitoring**. The system lacks explicit mention of **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, ROS) or **service meshes**, but the **modular model design** (e.g., swapping 2-parameter vs. 4-parameter models) hints at **plug-and-play extensibility**. The DT’s coordination is **deterministic** for control loops but may employ probabilistic methods (e.g., Bayesian inference) for uncertainty-aware calibration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context includes the following phases: **(1) Modeling Phase**: Abstract representations of the Physical Twin (PT) are created at varying levels of abstraction, such as CAD models (e.g., geometric and structural details of a car chassis) or dynamic models (e.g., differential equations for analyzing forces and movement). These models are built to understand specific aspects of the system, such as physical behavior or cyber components, and may be calibrated using algorithms to refine accuracy. **(2) Integration Phase**: Models are integrated into the DT framework, which combines **data** (collected via sensors from the PT over time), **models** (knowledge of cyber and physical aspects of the PT and its environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, optimizers). **(3) Deployment and Monitoring Phase**: The DT is deployed alongside the PT, where it continuously monitors the system’s performance using real-time sensor data. Calibration algorithms update the models dynamically to reflect the PT’s current state, ensuring alignment between the digital and physical systems. **(4) Decision and Adaptation Phase**: Monitors within the DT detect deviations (e.g., safety or performance violations) and trigger corrective actions, either autonomously via algorithms or through human intervention. The lifecycle also emphasizes **transition management** between phases, such as updating models with new data or reconfiguring algorithms to adapt to changing environmental conditions or system requirements. **Lifecycle governance** is implied through the iterative use of models and data to optimize the PT, though specific governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards for DT lifecycle) are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) emphasizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through structured methodologies:
  - **Fidelity Requirements**: Models must accurately represent the PT’s behavior under operating conditions. For example, the incubator’s **4-parameter thermal model** was selected over a 2-parameter model due to its superior prediction of **thermal inertia**, a critical physical property.
  - **Validation and Verification (V&V) Methods**:
    - **Experimental Validation**: Real-world data (e.g., sensor measurements from the incubator) is used to compare against model predictions, as demonstrated in the incubator case study where the 4-parameter model’s accuracy was empirically verified.
    - **Parameter Calibration**: Algorithms adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal coefficients) to minimize discrepancies between simulated and observed data, ensuring alignment with the PT’s actual behavior.
  - **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification**:
    - **Model Uncertainty**: Arises from simplifications (e.g., ignoring minor environmental factors in the incubator’s thermal model) or incomplete physical knowledge.
    - **Sensor Uncertainty**: Potential inaccuracies in measurements (e.g., temperature sensor noise) may propagate to model errors.
    - **Quantification**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied to involve statistical methods (e.g., error bounds, confidence intervals) to assess prediction reliability.
  - **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**:
    - **Monitors**: Continuously check for deviations between the PT’s state and model predictions (e.g., detecting temperature anomalies in the incubator).
    - **Feedback Loops**: Trigger corrective actions (e.g., recalibrating models or adjusting physical controls) when violations are detected.
  - **Quality Assurance Processes**:
    - **Iterative Refinement**: Models are updated based on new data and validation results (e.g., transitioning from a 2-parameter to a 4-parameter thermal model).
    - **Documentation and Traceability**: Ensures transparency in model development, validation, and deployment (e.g., open-source hardware/software in the incubator case study)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure for the incubator case study is not explicitly detailed in the documents, but the system implies a **local-area network (LAN) or direct cyber-physical connection** between the PT’s sensors/actuators (e.g., Raspberry Pi) and the DT’s computational components. Communication protocols are unspecified but may include **serial (UART), I2C, or Ethernet-based protocols** for sensor-actuator communication, with potential use of **MQTT or OPC UA** for industrial-grade DT deployments (not mentioned). The network topology is likely **star-shaped**, with the Raspberry Pi as the central node aggregating sensor data and dispatching control commands. Bandwidth and latency requirements are inferred to be **low** (e.g., sub-second delays for temperature readings) due to the system’s simplicity, but no quantitative thresholds are provided. Reliability mechanisms may include **redundant sensors** (e.g., two internal temperature sensors) and **fault-tolerant control algorithms** (e.g., fallback to manual override). Security protocols are not specified but could involve **basic authentication** for controller access or **encrypted communication** if deployed in an industrial setting."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin hosting deployment for the incubator case study is **on-premise**, with the Raspberry Pi serving as the primary computational node for both the PT’s controller and the DT’s simulation models. Computational requirements are modest, involving **real-time execution of differential equation models** (e.g., thermal dynamics) and **calibration algorithms** to update model parameters. Storage requirements are minimal, focusing on **time-series sensor data** and **model parameter logs**. Scalability mechanisms are not explicitly addressed, but the open-source repository suggests the system is designed for **modular expansion** (e.g., adding more sensors or models). Deployment models supported include **edge computing** (Raspberry Pi) and potentially **cloud integration** for larger-scale DTs, though the incubator’s simplicity prioritizes local deployment. Availability/reliability assurance is implied via **redundant hardware** (e.g., backup sensors) and **closed-loop control** to maintain operational safety, but no formal uptime guarantees or failover procedures are documented."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system integrates **cybersecurity protocols** and **safety mechanisms** to ensure reliable operation of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its Physical Twin (PT). The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the CPS and environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation. **Monitors** within the DT system are responsible for verifying safe and optimal performance, detecting violations, and triggering corrective actions—either through human intervention or automated algorithms. The system incorporates **fail-safe mechanisms** by enabling real-time adjustments to the PT when deviations are detected, thereby maintaining operational integrity. While the documents do not specify explicit **access control mechanisms**, **authentication systems**, or **threat mitigation strategies**, the DT framework inherently relies on **risk assessment approaches** to ensure the safety-critical nature of the system. The **supervisory controllers** and **state estimators** embedded in the DT further enhance safety by dynamically adapting to changes in the PT's operating conditions."
]
